On this page
# What is Quil?
[Quil](https://docs.rigetti.com/qcs/references/quil) — which stands for Quantum Instruction Language — is the language in which you'll write programs for execution on Rigetti QPUs. Quil is is an open standard governed by the [Quil-Lang organization](https://github.com/quil-lang/quil).
Quil is most easily written and executed by using the [Quil SDK](https://docs.rigetti.com/qcs#quil-sdk).
## Quil-T[​](https://docs.rigetti.com/qcs/guides/quil/quil#quil-t "Direct link to Quil-T")
[Quil-T](https://docs.rigetti.com/qcs/references/quil) is an extension of Quil which supports analog, pulse-level control. Quil-T is natively supported by Rigetti QPUs.
### Calibrations[​](https://docs.rigetti.com/qcs/guides/quil/quil#calibrations "Direct link to Calibrations")
Calibrations are a description of how a high-level Quil (or Quil-T) program should be transpiled into a series of analog pulse instructions. In particular, the calibrations that are provided by Rigetti are derived by a careful process of tuning various parameters to maximize the execution quality for the target QPU.
Within QCS, all Quil programs (even those not using Quil-T syntax) are transpiled to a Quil-T pulse program as a step in the assembly process. When you compile your Quil program for execution on a QPU, the most current calibrations available for the QPU will be used to calibrate your program. You can override these calibrations by supplying calibrations explicitly within your program in the form of `DEFFRAME`, `DEFWAVEFORM`, and `DEFCAL` blocks. This support for user-calibrated programs gives you more precise control over how your programs are executed on our QPUs.
Frame definitions (`DEFFRAME` blocks) may not be modified within programs, but must be included verbatim as provided in the Rigetti calibrations.
#### Inspection using the QCS CLI[​](https://docs.rigetti.com/qcs/guides/quil/quil#inspection-using-the-qcs-cli "Direct link to Inspection using the QCS CLI")
If you'd like to inspect Quil-T calibrations for a QPU, you can use the QCS API's [get-quilt-calibrations](https://docs.api.qcs.rigetti.com/#operation/GetQuiltCalibrations) endpoint. To call this endpoint using the [QCS CLI](https://docs.rigetti.com/qcs/references/qcs-cli), use the [`qcs api`](https://docs.rigetti.com/qcs/references/qcs-cli#api-commands) command. For instance, to get calibrations for `Aspen-11`, run:

```


qcs api get-quilt-calibrations Aspen-11  



```

If you have [`jq`](https://github.com/stedolan/jq) installed, you can get more human-readable output with:

```


qcs api get-quilt-calibrations Aspen-11 | jq -r '.quilt'  



```

#### Inspection using pyQuil[​](https://docs.rigetti.com/qcs/guides/quil/quil#inspection-using-pyquil "Direct link to Inspection using pyQuil")
To inspect Quil-T calibrations for a QPU using [pyQuil](https://docs.rigetti.com/qcs/references/pyquil), you can use the `calibration_program` property on `QPUCompiler`. For instance, to get calibrations for `Aspen-11`, write:

```


from pyquil import get_qc  





  





qc = get_qc("Aspen-11")  





  





print(qc.compiler.calibration_program)  



```

## Other Languages[​](https://docs.rigetti.com/qcs/guides/quil/quil#other-languages "Direct link to Other Languages")
OpenQASM v2 may be transpiled to Quil using the [Quil compiler](https://docs.rigetti.com/qcs/references/quilc).
[Previous Language Support](https://docs.rigetti.com/qcs/guides/quil/language-support)[Next The Lifecycle of a Program](https://docs.rigetti.com/qcs/guides/the-lifecycle-of-a-program)

  * [Other Languages](https://docs.rigetti.com/qcs/guides/quil/quil#other-languages)

href="/qcs/guides/quil/"><span>Quil</span></a></li><li class="breadcrumbs__item breadcrumbs__item--active"><span class="breadcrumbs__link">What is Quil?</span></li></ul></nav><div class="tocCollapsible_ETCw theme-doc-toc-mobile tocMobile_ITEo"><button type="button" class="clean-btn tocCollapsibleButton_TO0P">On this page</button></div><div class="theme-doc-markdown markdown"><header><h1>What is Quil?</h1></header><p><a class="" href="/qcs/references/quil">Quil</a> — which stands for Quantum Instruction Language — is the language in which you'll write programs for execution on Rigetti QPUs. Quil is is an open standard governed by the <a href="https://github.com/quil-lang/quil" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">Quil-Lang organization</a>.</p>
<p>Quil is most easily written and executed by using the <a class="" href="/qcs#quil-sdk">Quil SDK</a>.</p>
<h2 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_Vzrq" id="quil-t">Quil-T<a href="#quil-t" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Quil-T" title="Direct link to Quil-T" translate="no">​</a></h2>
<p><a class="" href="/qcs/references/quil">Quil-T</a> is an extension of Quil which supports analog, pulse-level control. Quil-T is natively supported by Rigetti QPUs.</p>
<h3 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_Vzrq" id="calibrations">Calibrations<a href="#calibrations" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Calibrations" title="Direct link to Calibrations" translate="no">​</a></h3>
<p>Calibrations are a description of how a high-level Quil (or Quil-T) program should be transpiled into a series of analog pulse instructions. In particular, the calibrations that are provided by Rigetti are derived by a careful process of tuning various parameters to maximize the execution quality for the target QPU.</p>
<p>Within QCS, all Quil programs (even those not using Quil-T syntax) are transpiled to a Quil-T pulse program as a step in the assembly process. When you compile your Quil program for execution on a QPU, the most current calibrations available for the QPU will be used to calibrate your program. You can override these calibrations by supplying calibrations explicitly within your program in the form of <code>DEFFRAME</code>, <code>DEFWAVEFORM</code>, and <code>DEFCAL</code> blocks. This support for user-calibrated programs gives you more precise control over how your programs are executed on our QPUs.</p>
<p>Frame definitions (<code>DEFFRAME</code> blocks) may not be modified within programs, but must be included verbatim as provided in the Rigetti calibrations.</p>
<h4 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_Vzrq" id="inspection-using-the-qcs-cli">Inspection using the QCS CLI<a href="#inspection-using-the-qcs-cli" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Inspection using the QCS CLI" title="Direct link to Inspection using the QCS CLI" translate="no">​</a></h4>
<p>If you'd like to inspect Quil-T calibrations for a QPU, you can use the QCS API's <a href="https://docs.api.qcs.rigetti.com/#operation/GetQuiltCalibrations" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">get-quilt-calibrations</a> endpoint. To call this endpoint using the <a class="" href="/qcs/references/qcs-cli">QCS CLI</a>, use the <a class="" href="/qcs/references/qcs-cli#api-commands"><code>qcs api</code></a> command. For instance, to get calibrations for <code>Aspen-11</code>, run:</p>
<div class="language-sh codeBlockContainer_Ckt0 theme-code-block" style="--prism-color: #393A34; --prism-background-color: #f6f8fa;"><div class="codeBlockContent_QJqH"><pre tabindex="0" class="prism-code language-sh codeBlock_bY9V thin-scrollbar" style="color: rgb(57, 58, 52); background-color: rgb(246, 248, 250);"><code class="codeBlockLines_e6Vv"><div class="token-line" style="color: rgb(57, 58, 52);"><span class="token plain">qcs api get-quilt-calibrations Aspen-11</span><br></div></code></pre><div class="buttonGroup_M5ko"><button type="button" aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" title="Copy" class="clean-btn"><span class="copyButtonIcons_IEyt" aria-hidden="true"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="copyButtonIcon_TrPX"><path fill="currentColor" d="M19,21H8V7H19M19,5H8A2,2 0 0,0 6,7V21A2,2 0 0,0 8,23H19A2,2 0 0,0 21,21V7A2,2 0 0,0 19,5M16,1H4A2,2 0 0,0 2,3V17H4V3H16V1Z"></path></svg><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="copyButtonSuccessIcon_cVMy"><path fill="currentColor" d="M21,7L9,19L3.5,13.5L4.91,12.09L9,16.17L19.59,5.59L21,7Z"></path></svg></span></button></div></div></div>
<div class="theme-admonition theme-admonition-tip admonition_xJq3 alert alert--success"><div class="admonitionHeading_Gvgb"><span class="admonitionIcon_Rf37"><svg viewBox="0 0 12 16"><path fill-rule="evenodd" d="M6.5 0C3.48 0 1 2.19 1 5c0 .92.55 2.25 1 3 1.34 2.25 1.78 2.78 2 4v1h5v-1c.22-1.22.66-1.75 2-4 .45-.75 1-2.08 1-3 0-2.81-2.48-5-5.5-5zm3.64 7.48c-.25.44-.47.8-.67 1.11-.86 1.41-1.25 2.06-1.45 3.23-.02.05-.02.11-.02.17H5c0-.06 0-.13-.02-.17-.2-1.17-.59-1.83-1.45-3.23-.2-.31-.42-.67-.67-1.11C2.44 6.78 2 5.65 2 5c0-2.2 2.02-4 4.5-4 1.22 0 2.36.42 3.22 1.19C10.55 2.94 11 3.94 11 5c0 .66-.44 1.78-.86 2.48zM4 14h5c-.23 1.14-1.3 2-2.5 2s-2.27-.86-2.5-2z"></path></svg></span>tip</div><div class="admonitionContent_BuS1"><p>If you have <a href="https://github.com/stedolan/jq" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class=""><code>jq</code></a> installed, you can get more human-readable output with:</p><div class="language-sh codeBlockContainer_Ckt0 theme-code-block" style="--prism-color: #393A34; --prism-background-color: #f6f8fa;"><div class="codeBlockContent_QJqH"><pre tabindex="0" class="prism-code language-sh codeBlock_bY9V thin-scrollbar" style="color: rgb(57, 58, 52); background-color: rgb(246, 248, 250);"><code class="codeBlockLines_e6Vv"><div class="token-line" style="color: rgb(57, 58, 52);"><span class="token plain">qcs api get-quilt-calibrations Aspen-11 </span><span class="token operator" style="color: rgb(57, 58, 52);">|</span><span class="token plain"> jq </span><span class="token parameter variable" style="color: rgb(54, 172, 170);">-r</span><span class="token plain"> </span><span class="token string" style="color: rgb(227, 17, 108);">'.quilt'</span><br></div></code></pre><div class="buttonGroup_M5ko"><button type="button" aria-label="Toggle word wrap" title="Toggle word wrap" class="clean-btn"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="wordWrapButtonIcon_b1P5" aria-hidden="true"><path fill="currentColor" d="M4 19h6v-2H4v2zM20 5H4v2h16V5zm-3 6H4v2h13.25c1.1 0 2 .9 2 2s-.9 2-2 2H15v-2l-3 3l3 3v-2h2c2.21 0 4-1.79 4-4s-1.79-4-4-4z"></path></svg></button><button type="button" aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" title="Copy" class="clean-btn"><span class="copyButtonIcons_IEyt" aria-hidden="true"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="copyButtonIcon_TrPX"><path fill="currentColor" d="M19,21H8V7H19M19,5H8A2,2 0 0,0 6,7V21A2,2 0 0,0 8,23H19A2,2 0 0,0 21,21V7A2,2 0 0,0 19,5M16,1H4A2,2 0 0,0 2,3V17H4V3H16V1Z"></path></svg><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="copyButtonSuccessIcon_cVMy"><path fill="currentColor" d="M21,7L9,19L3.5,13.5L4.91,12.09L9,16.17L19.59,5.59L21,7Z"></path></svg></span></button></div></div></div></div></div>
<h4 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_Vzrq" id="inspection-using-pyquil">Inspection using pyQuil<a href="#inspection-using-pyquil" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Inspection using pyQuil" title="Direct link to Inspection using pyQuil" translate="no">​</a></h4>
<p>To inspect Quil-T calibrations for a QPU using <a class="" href="/qcs/references/pyquil">pyQuil</a>, you can use the <code>calibration_program</code> property on <code>QPUCompiler</code>. For instance, to get calibrations for <code>Aspen-11</code>, write:</p>

<div class="language-py codeBlockContainer_Ckt0 theme-code-block" style="--prism-color: #393A34; --prism-background-color: #f6f8fa;"><div class="codeBlockContent_QJqH"><pre tabindex="0" class="prism-code language-py codeBlock_bY9V thin-scrollbar" style="color: rgb(57, 58, 52); background-color: rgb(246, 248, 250);"><code class="codeBlockLines_e6Vv"><div class="token-line" style="color: rgb(57, 58, 52);"><span class="token keyword" style="color: rgb(0, 0, 159);">from</span><span class="token plain"> pyquil </span><span class="token keyword" style="color: rgb(0, 0, 159);">import</span><span class="token plain"> get_qc</span><br></div><div class="token-line" style="color: rgb(57, 58, 52);"><span class="token plain" style="display: inline-block;"></span><br></div><div class="token-line" style="color: rgb(57, 58, 52);"><span class="token plain">qc </span><span class="token operator" style="color: rgb(57, 58, 52);">=</span><span class="token plain"> get_qc</span><span class="token punctuation" style="color: rgb(57, 58, 52);">(</span><span class="token string" style="color: rgb(227, 17, 108);">"Aspen-11"</span><span class="token punctuation" style="color: rgb(57, 58, 52);">)</span><span class="token plain"></span><br></div><div class="token-line" style="color: rgb(57, 58, 52);"><span class="token plain" style="display: inline-block;"></span><br></div><div class="token-line" style="color: rgb(57, 58, 52);"><span class="token plain"></span><span class="token keyword" style="color: rgb(0, 0, 159);">print</span><span class="token punctuation" style="color: rgb(57, 58, 52);">(</span><span class="token plain">qc</span><span class="token punctuation" style="color: rgb(57, 58, 52);">.</span><span class="token plain">compiler</span><span class="token punctuation" style="color: rgb(57, 58, 52);">.</span><span class="token plain">calibration_program</span><span class="token punctuation" style="color: rgb(57, 58, 52);">)</span><br></div></code></pre><div class="buttonGroup_M5ko"><button type="button" aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" title="Copy" class="clean-btn"><span 21,21V7A2,2 0 0,0 19,5M16,1H4A2,2 0 0,0 2,3V17H4V3H16V1Z"></path></svg><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="copyButtonSuccessIcon_cVMy"><path fill="currentColor" <h2 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_Vzrq" id="other-languages">Other Languages<a href="#other-languages" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Other Languages" title="Direct link to Other Languages" translate="no">​</a></h2>
<p>OpenQASM v2 may be transpiled to Quil using the <a class="" href="/qcs/references/quilc">Quil compiler</a>.</p></div></article><nav class="docusaurus-mt-lg pagination-nav" aria-label="Docs pages"><a class="pagination-nav__link pagination-nav__link--prev" href="/qcs/guides/quil/language-support"><div class="pagination-nav__sublabel">Previous</div><div class="pagination-nav__label">Language Support</div></a><a class="pagination-nav__link pagination-nav__link--next" href="/qcs/guides/the-lifecycle-of-a-program"><div class="pagination-nav__sublabel">Next</div><div class="pagination-nav__label">The Lifecycle of a Program</div></a></nav></div></div><div class="col col--3"><div class="tableOfContents_bqdL thin-scrollbar theme-doc-toc-desktop"><ul class="table-of-contents table-of-contents__left-border"><li><a href="#quil-t" class="table-of-contents__link toc-highlight">Quil-T</a><ul><li><a href="#calibrations" class="table-of-contents__link toc-highlight">Calibrations</a></li></ul></li><li><a href="#other-languages" class="table-of-contents__link toc-highlight">Other Languages</a></li></ul></div></div></div></div></main></div></div></div><footer class="theme-layout-footer footer"><div class="container container-fluid"><div class="footer__bottom text--center"><div class="footer__copyright">Copyright © 2026 Rigetti Computing.</div></div></div></footer></div></body></html>
